tool.name: ANGSD X-Contamination
tool.description: Estimate nuclear contamination on X chromosome
tool.command:
## 1. Create symlinks so the C++ tool recognizes them as gzipped files ln -s '$icnts_file' counts.icnts.gz && ln -s '$hapmap_file' hapmap.gz &&
## 2. Run the contamination tool using the symlinks contamination -a counts.icnts.gz -h hapmap.gz 2> '${sample_name}.X.contamination.out' &&
## 3. Run the EAGER python parser python3 '$__tool_directory__/print_x_contamination.py' '${sample_name}.X.contamination.out'

param.name: icnts_file
param.label: ANGSD Internal Counts (.icnts.gz)
param.help: Connect the 'Internal Counts' output from the main ANGSD tool here.

param.name: hapmap_file
param.label: HapMap ChrX Reference
param.help: Provide the HapMapChrX.gz file required for contamination estimation.

param.name: sample_name
param.label: Sample Name
param.help: This will appear in your MultiQC report tables.

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool takes the internal counts (`.icnts.gz`) generated by **ANGSD** and estimates the nuclear contamination on the X chromosome for male samples. It uses the `contamination` binary bundled with ANGSD and uses the `nf-core/eager` python script to format the output perfectly for **MultiQC**.
**Inputs** 1. **Internal Counts:** The binary output from the ANGSD tool (run with `-iCounts 1` and `-doCounts 1`). 2. **HapMap File:** A specialized reference file (e.g., `HapMapChrX.gz`). 3. **Sample Name:** The name you want to see in the MultiQC table.
